title: Fix false cancel match from "enough" keyword in ChatInterpreter deterministic pattern
description:
## Problem
The deterministic pattern matcher `ChatInterpreter.ParseIntent` has a cancel regex that includes the word "enough":
```csharp
Regex.IsMatch(message, @"\b(stop|cancel|enough|quit|abort|nevermind|never mind)\b", RegexOptions.IgnoreCase)
```

This causes a false positive whenever a player naturally says "enough" in a non-cancellation context, e.g. "Is this enough to play Minecraft?" → matched as "cancel" → emergency stop fired.

## Evidence
From session log 2026-06-27:
```
[10:02:37] [chat] <TheMasonX23> What do you have access to? Is this enough to play minecraft?
[10:02:37] [chat] <TheMasonX23> -> cancel
[10:02:37] [stop] emergency stop dispatched to adapter via StopNow
```
This repeated 3 times (10:02:37, 10:02:50, 10:03:07) before the LLM path finally handled it as "status" at 10:03:07.

## Recommended Fix
Remove "enough" from the cancel regex. The word "enough" is too common in everyday language (e.g. "is this enough", "that's enough food", "enough wood") to be a reliable cancel signal. Consider replacing with a more specific pattern like `"that.s enough"` or `"(that's|thats) enough"` if needed, or just remove it entirely since "stop", "cancel", "abort", "quit", "nevermind" already cover the real cancel intents.

## Files
- `Agent.Planning/ChatInterpreter.cs` line ~280: cancel regex pattern

## Priority
High — this causes repeated false emergency stops that disrupt the agent and confuse users.
